novel_features.name: Ranked best-of search
novel_features.description: Search a place type, auto-fetch details for the top hits, and return them ranked by rating, review count, or Tripadvisor ranking in one table.
novel_features.rationale: Requires fanning out N detail calls with a bounded scan cap and sorting locally; a thin API wrapper returns name-only stubs and makes the agent rank by hand.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the task is 'find the best X in Y' instead of calling find then show ten times yourself.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side compare
novel_features.description: Pull details (and subratings + trip-type mix) for 2-5 location IDs and emit one structured comparison table.
novel_features.rationale: Joins details across multiple cached/live records into a single view; the API exposes each location only one call at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to decide between specific places an agent already shortlisted.

novel_features.name: Geo-ranked shortlist
novel_features.description: From a lat/long, find nearby places, batch-fetch details up to a scan cap, filter by category and minimum rating, return the top K.
novel_features.rationale: Composes the nearby endpoint with a bounded detail fan-out plus local filter/sort; doing this by hand burns many agent turns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for 'best-rated <type> near here' from coordinates.

novel_features.name: Rating drift
novel_features.description: Compare a location's stored rating/review-count snapshot against a fresh fetch and report the delta, flagging drops past a threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible with a historical snapshot in the local store; the live API has no versioning and thin wrappers keep no state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to detect whether a place you tracked has slipped since you last checked.

novel_features.name: Location digest
novel_features.description: One location ID to a single agent-friendly payload combining details, top reviews (user-generated), and photo URLs.
novel_features.rationale: Composes details + reviews + photos into one response so an agent makes one call instead of three.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need the full picture of one place without three round trips.

novel_features.name: Traveler fit ranking
novel_features.description: Rank search results by how well their trip-type mix matches a declared traveler profile (families, couples, solo, business).
novel_features.rationale: Weights the trip_types distribution (only surfaced in details) across N fetched records; no thin wrapper accumulates or weights this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to bias a shortlist toward who is actually traveling.

narrative.headline: Every Tripadvisor Content API endpoint, plus a local store and ranked compare/best/drift commands no other Tripadvisor tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Search hotels, restaurants, and attractions and get rating, review count, and ranking up front so an agent can rank and choose. Beyond the raw five endpoints, `best` and `nearby-best` search-then-rank in one call, `compare` puts places side by side, and `drift` flags a rating that slipped since you last looked — all backed by a local SQLite cache so repeat lookups are free and offline.
narrative.auth_narrative: Uses an official Tripadvisor Content API key passed as the `key` query parameter (set TRIPADVISOR_API_KEY). Create a key at tripadvisor.com/developers; you must add an IP restriction (your public IPv4 as a /32) or a domain restriction before the key is shown. Domain-restricted keys also require a Referer header on every request.

quickstart.comment: Check the key is set and the API is reachable before anything else
Search by name to get a location_id
Full details: rating, review count, ranking, address, price level
Search and rank the top hotels by rating in one call
Put two places side by side for a decision

troubleshoots.fix: Set TRIPADVISOR_API_KEY, and confirm the key's IP restriction matches your current public IPv4 (it changes on many networks).
Domain-restricted keys require a Referer header; switch the key to an IP restriction for CLI use, or set the configured Referer.

troubleshoots.symptom: reviews or photos returns only a few items
troubleshoots.fix: The free Content API hard-caps reviews and photos at 5 per location; this is expected, not a bug.
You hit the daily budget or 50 req/s limit; budgets reset at midnight UTC. Cached locations still serve offline.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to search Tripadvisor places and compare them by rating, review count, or ranking — picking the best hotel/restaurant/attraction in a city or near a point, putting specific options side by side, or pulling a place's details, recent traveler reviews, and photos. The local cache makes repeat comparisons fast and offline.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to book hotels, tables, or tours — the Content API is read-only and this tool performs no bookings.
Do not use it to fetch full review histories; the free Content API returns at most 5 recent reviews per location.
Do not use it to scrape tripadvisor.com directly; that is bot-protected and out of scope.
Do not use it to summarize or judge review sentiment; it returns the raw user-generated text for the agent to interpret.

recipes.title: Best-rated restaurants in a city
recipes.explanation: Searches, auto-fetches details for the top hits, and returns them ranked by rating with review counts.

recipes.title: Decide between two hotels
recipes.explanation: Pulls both detail records and narrows the comparison to the fields that drive the choice.

recipes.title: Best near a coordinate
recipes.explanation: Finds nearby attractions, ranks the highly-rated ones, all from a lat/long.

recipes.title: Has this place slipped?
recipes.explanation: Compares the cached rating snapshot against a fresh fetch and flags a meaningful drop.

recipes.title: One-call full picture
recipes.explanation: Combines details, recent reviews, and photos in a single payload narrowed to what you need.

trigger_phrases: best hotels in Paris on tripadvisor
